title: Simultaneous Non-Vanishing of L-Functions at the Central Point
speaker: Alexandra Florea
abstract: In this talk, I will focus on simultaneous non-vanishing results for Dirichlet L-functions at the central point 1/2. Specifically, I will describe non-vanishing results (conditional on GRH) for two L-functions associated to Dirichlet characters in the same Galois orbit, and simultaneous non-vanishing of four L-functions as we vary over characters \chi modulo q, conditional on GRH and the Ramanujan-Petterson conjecture. This is based on joint works with Bui-Ngo and Bui-Milinovich.

proof_stages.label: Nonvanishing philosophy and simultaneous question
proof_stages.summary: The talk frames Chowla-type central nonvanishing for Dirichlet L-functions, reviews known statistical nonvanishing proportions, and motivates simultaneous nonvanishing by comparison with automorphic-form results and questions about forced versus unexplained zeros.

proof_stages.label: Zacharias triple-nonvanishing template
proof_stages.summary: Zacharias's result for three Dirichlet L-functions is presented as the preceding template: a classical mollifier and Hölder reduce nonvanishing to a mollified cubic moment and fourth moment, with the hard part tied to root-number oscillation and hyper-Kloosterman-type sums.

proof_stages.label: Conditional quadruple theorem and Hölder reduction
proof_stages.summary: The new GRH-conditional theorem is stated for four fixed primitive twists of small squarefree pairwise coprime moduli, and Hölder reduces the desired positive proportion to a lower bound for a mollified fourth moment and a sharp upper bound for a mollified sixth moment.

proof_stages.label: GRH-guided Harper mollifier and sharp moment bounds
proof_stages.summary: Using a GRH inequality bounding log L by short prime sums, the mollifier is built as a product over many prime intervals with truncated Taylor expansions, yielding sharp q-sized upper bounds for mollified moments, in particular the sixth moment.

proof_stages.label: Twisted fourth moment and diagonal lower bound
proof_stages.summary: An unconditional asymptotic for the required twisted fourth moment is developed via the approximate functional equation and character orthogonality, while after inserting the new mollifier GRH-controlled short Euler products cancel the diagonal L(1)-factors to give a q-sized lower bound and make lower-order main terms negligible.

proof_stages.label: Off-diagonal analysis and swap terms
proof_stages.summary: The off-diagonal terms in the twisted fourth moment are controlled by separating unbalanced and balanced ranges, applying Voronoi summation, Cauchy and exponential-sum estimates, and the delta method, with the remaining one-swap and two-swap main terms identified and shown harmless after mollification.

examples.name: Sign-forced zero from root number -1
examples.note: A functional-equation sign of -1 is used as the standard trivial mechanism forcing vanishing at the central point, motivating the claim that primitive Dirichlet twists should otherwise not vanish.
examples.engagement_reason: the root-number -1 case is stated as a trivial source of central vanishing without deriving it from the functional equation

passes.reason: root number -1 forces an odd functional equation and hence a central zero, obstructing naive central nonvanishing
